archetype: Cryo-Electron Microscopy Instrument Calibration and Data Integrity Orchestration System
description: Orchestrates nanometer-scale calibration, automated data acquisition, and cryptographic audit trails for cryo-EM instruments used in pharmaceutical R&D; ensures data integrity for atomic-resolution protein structure determination across distributed lab networks.
rules: MUST enforce MRC/CCP4 image format standards and EMDB deposition validation protocols
MUST maintain ISO 17025-compliant calibration logs with immutable timestamps
MUST integrate with microscope vendor-specific APIs (Thermo Fisher SerialEM, Gatan SerialEM) for hardware control
MUST implement 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance for FDA submissions
MUST synchronize atomic clock time sources across all instruments for cross-site experiment correlation
anti_patterns: DO NOT use generic image databases—cryo-EM micrographs require specialized MRC2000 format support and metadata embedding
DO NOT rely on manual calibration logs—nanometer-scale drift requires sub-second automated sensor fusion from vacuum/temperature/position sensors
DO NOT ignore beam-induced motion correction—failing to implement MotionCor2-style algorithms invalidates structural resolution claims
key_technical_challenges: Real-time sensor fusion from 50+ hardware channels (liquid nitrogen levels, beam alignment, stage position) with sub-millisecond latency
Terabyte-scale image pipeline integrity verification using cryptographic hashing of every frame before computational processing
Multi-instrument calibration drift prediction using Bayesian modeling of environmental variables across geographically distributed labs

recommended_stack.language: Python + Cython—dominates scientific computing ecosystem with libraries like NumPy, SciPy, and cryo-EM specific tools (cryoSPARC, RELION bindings)
recommended_stack.framework: FastAPI—async-first for real-time instrument telemetry ingestion and low-latency control loops
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB—time-series calibration data with ACID compliance for audit trails
recommended_stack.cache: Redis—sub-millisecond caching of calibration parameters and instrument state
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Pulsar—guaranteed delivery for critical calibration events with tiered storage for raw image metadata
special_services: MRC format validator with EMDB schema compliance
Hardware abstraction layer for vendor API unification
